summary: Iteration 13: Tested real MQTT broker integration (broker.emqx.io:1883). All 16 MQTT backend API tests passed. Frontend shows MQTT Connected status and device/zone controls successfully publish MQTT commands. Device simulator connects and subscribes to command topics. Previous features (booking flow, pricing rules, admin) working correctly.

minor.issue: Returns 404 instead of empty array when no bookings exist

integration_issues.issue: WebSocket shows 'closed before connection established' warning when navigating quickly - race condition but works after page settles

passed_tests: POST /api/iot/devices/{id}/control - Turn on device via MQTT (200)
POST /api/iot/devices/{id}/control - Turn off device via MQTT (200)
POST /api/iot/zones/{id}/control - Zone All On via MQTT (200)
POST /api/iot/zones/{id}/control - Zone All Off via MQTT (200)
POST /api/iot/devices - Create device with MQTT topic (200)
GET /api/iot/devices - List devices with MQTT topics (200)
WebSocket /api/iot/ws - Endpoint exists and accepts connections
frontend_tests_passed: IoT Dashboard loads with MQTT status indicator showing 'MQTT Connected • broker.emqx.io'
7 device cards displayed with online/offline status
Device toggle switch sends MQTT command (verified in backend logs)
Zones tab shows 3 zones with device counts
Zone All On button works - 'Zone activated' toast displayed
Energy tab shows analytics charts (2 recharts)
Stats bar shows Online/Active/Power Draw/Avg Daily Cost

mqtt_verification.backend_logs_confirm: MQTT published to horizon/devices/... and horizon/powerplay/turf1/... topics
mqtt_verification.device_simulator: Connects to broker and subscribes to command topics successfully

critical_code_review_comments: WebSocket race condition on quick navigation - consider adding reconnection logic with backoff
seed_data_creation: TEST_ prefixed devices created and deleted during pytest run
context_for_next_testing_agent: MQTT broker integration is LIVE (not mocked) - connects to broker.emqx.io:1883. Backend successfully publishes commands to MQTT topics. Device simulator can be run with: cd /app/backend && python device_simulator.py. WebSocket endpoint /api/iot/ws works for real-time updates. All 7 seeded devices and 3 zones work with real MQTT.

verified_mqtt_features.device_control_mqtt: VERIFIED - commands published to MQTT topics
verified_mqtt_features.zone_control_mqtt: VERIFIED - bulk commands to all zone devices
verified_mqtt_features.device_simulator: VERIFIED - connects and subscribes to command topics
verified_mqtt_features.telemetry_subscription: VERIFIED - backend subscribes to horizon/+/+/status and telemetry
